rationale: A complete WMI persistence triad was created on host-01: an event filter (EV-001) triggers on logon of 'user-02', a CommandLineEventConsumer (A0) executes a base64-encoded PowerShell payload, and the filter-to-consumer binding (EV-002) ties them together — confirming a fully operational WMI subscription backdoor. DER-001 decodes the payload as a PowerShell cradle that disables SSL certificate validation and downloads a remote script from an internal IP (192.168.0.4:443/GoPro5/black/2018/_rp) for immediate execution via IEX, a classic fileless stager pattern. The consumer name 'WindowsParentalControlMigration' is a deceptive masquerade of a legitimate Windows component. The discrepancy in user context between filter/consumer creation (corp\user-02) and the binding (user-01) may indicate lateral movement or credential abuse, warranting immediate host isolation.
